title: Paper Lanterns

sections.description: The lantern song in its plainest shape.
The tune opens outward and starts to glow.
The procession is heard from the river rather than seen.
The lanterns come back into view only after a full breath.
Final lantern landing at the doorway.

insights.message: Counterline cell returns in bloom and homecoming.
46 symbolic timing placements were normalized into absolute beat events.

rhythm.summary: 100% of note events carry rhythm roles, 100% anchor downbeat presence, 0 offbeat-only bars, cadence contract satisfied
insights: Rhythmic bar roles cover 16 of 16 bars.
Anchor voice articulates the downbeat in 100% of its active bars.
Every active bar retains at least one strong-beat articulation.
Cadence contract satisfied at bar 16, beat 1.

validationWarnings.message: The phrase packs many note attacks into a bar, which may read as fussy in exposed oscillator textures.
Simultaneous upper-register intervals look harsh enough to deserve manual listening checks.
There are several simultaneous collision points, which may translate into exposed roughness.

suggestedMusicalEdits: Shorten or re-register the bell accent. Focus on bar 4, beat 15.50 first.
Thin the densest bar or two first, especially ornamental attacks that sit on top of already-held harmony.
Smooth the sharpest section change with a shared pickup, common tone, or gentler density handoff if the transition feels too abrupt.
Differentiate statement/arrival bars from continuation bars rhythmically so returns feel planted instead of merely repeated.
